team_wide_tips.tip: Play for mid-board control, not alpha strikes
team_wide_tips.detail: Your low model count and high durability mean you win by owning central objectives and forcing fights on your terms, not by trading early operatives at range.

team_wide_tips.tip: Advance in a tight but staggered formation
team_wide_tips.detail: Move up together in TP1, using cover and obscuring to deny clean shots, but keep 2" spacing to avoid multi-target blast and splash attacks.

team_wide_tips.tip: Use ploys to reward standing still or minimal movement
team_wide_tips.detail: Plan activations so key hitters either don't move or move less than 3" before they shoot or fight, to maximise bonuses from your core Strategic Ploys.

team_wide_tips.tip: Value every body – avoid pointless trades
team_wide_tips.detail: With 5–6 operatives, losing one early is a huge percentage of your force; only expose a model if it meaningfully advances scoring or removes a key enemy threat.

team_wide_tips.tip: Abuse durability to sit on objectives under fire
team_wide_tips.detail: When choosing between perfect cover and being on an objective, you can often afford to stand on the point in cover or conceal, forcing the opponent to overcommit damage.

team_wide_tips.tip: Plan your grenade and blast turns
team_wide_tips.detail: Hold key blast weapons and frag/krak grenades for high-value clusters or to crack elite 10-wound models; avoid wasting them on single weak targets.
Position so that if an enemy charges or overextends to finish a wounded Plague Marine, you can immediately counter-charge or double fight with a nearby operative.

team_wide_tips.tip: Choose Tac Ops that reward staying alive
team_wide_tips.detail: Favour secondaries that score off mid-board control, surviving specialists, or late-game positioning rather than those demanding early risky aggression.
Pre-plan paths to objectives and key vantage points; a mis-positioned operative may need multiple turning points to become relevant again.

team_wide_tips.tip: Use Poxwalkers (if available) as screens and action bots
team_wide_tips.detail: Let cheap bodies do risky mission actions, screen charges, and body-block lanes while Plague Marines focus on killing and holding key points.

elite_marines_and_chaos.detail: Force brawls where you can double fight and leverage abilities that boost melee output, aiming to remove expensive enemy elites in one activation.

elite_marines_and_chaos.tip: Focus fire on one threat at a time
elite_marines_and_chaos.detail: Stack shooting and melee onto a single high-value operative rather than spreading damage, ensuring you fully remove their key guns or leaders.
Accept some chip damage and prioritize trades where you lose an operative only if it takes at least one enemy elite with it or swings scoring heavily.

horde_teams.detail: Push up fast behind cover and force the horde to fight you around central objectives where they cannot ignore your presence.
Target clumps with blast, splash, and grenades; even if a single attack does not kill, spreading damage softens multiple bodies for follow-up.
Target fast or mission-action capable enemy bodies early to limit their ability to flood objectives and score secondaries.

shooty_teams_with_good_range.tip: Use terrain and obscuring ploys to cross the board
shooty_teams_with_good_range.detail: Chain movement from cover to cover, use obscuring-style ploys, and avoid open lanes until you are close enough to threaten charges or short-range shots.
Occasionally leave one durable operative mildly exposed on or near an objective to tempt the opponent into stepping out of safety, then punish with return fire or charges.

shooty_teams_with_good_range.tip: Target special weapons and vantage holders
shooty_teams_with_good_range.detail: Prioritize eliminating enemy operatives with high-AP guns and those on vantage points, even if it costs tempo, to reduce incoming damage long-term.

melee_heavy_teams.tip: Make them charge through your threat zone
melee_heavy_teams.detail: Hold positions just beyond their threat range so that if they commit to a long charge or dash-charge, you have follow-up charges ready.
Use cheaper or less critical operatives to absorb initial charges while your key melee fighters stand just behind them ready to swing back.
Stay under movement thresholds or rely on retaliation to gain rerolls, increasing the chance that your counter-attacks delete charging enemies.

elite_shooting_teams.tip: Close to mid-range and force trades
elite_shooting_teams.detail: Use terrain and ploys to reach mid-range, where your toughness and short-range firepower let you trade their expensive gunners for your brawlers.

elite_shooting_teams.tip: Protect your leader and key gunners
elite_shooting_teams.detail: Keep the Champion and important specialists out of sight from enemy snipers and plasma until you can either delete or pin those guns.
If an enemy alpha strike fails to kill a targeted Plague Marine, immediately punish the exposed attackers instead of retreating.

sneaky_and_infiltration_teams.tip: Guard flanks and backfield with a flexible operative
sneaky_and_infiltration_teams.detail: Leave one Plague Marine watching likely entry points or concealed paths to deter infiltrators scoring free secondaries.

sneaky_and_infiltration_teams.tip: Sit durable bodies on critical objectives
sneaky_and_infiltration_teams.detail: Place your toughest operatives on mission-critical points so even if infiltrators appear nearby, they cannot easily steal scoring without overcommitting.

sneaky_and_infiltration_teams.tip: Use blast and templates when they uncloak
sneaky_and_infiltration_teams.detail: When sneaky operatives finally reveal themselves to charge or shoot, try to catch them with grenades or blast weapons while they are clustered.

champion.tip: Equip high-impact melee and a strong pistol
champion.detail: Loadouts like power fist plus plasma pistol let the Champion threaten both elite and multi-wound targets at range and in melee.

champion.tip: Preserve the Champion for mid-game swings
champion.detail: Avoid exposing the Champion turn one; instead, deliver them into combat once the enemy has already committed and you can delete 1–2 operatives in a single activation.

melee_brutes.detail: Point melee specialists at contested objectives to bully enemy operatives off them through charges and double fight actions.

melee_brutes.tip: Avoid charging into unsupported enemy lines
melee_brutes.detail: Only charge where you can either survive the counter-attack or follow up with support from another operative in the same or next activation.

ranged_specialists.tip: Target key specialists rather than chaff
ranged_specialists.detail: Use long-range or special weapons to remove enemy leaders, gunners, and mission-critical operatives instead of generic bodies.

ranged_specialists.tip: Anchor fire lanes rather than roaming
ranged_specialists.detail: Park gunners on firing lanes that cover key objectives and routes, moving only when necessary to maintain angles and cover.

poxwalkers_and_cheap_bodies.tip: Screen and zone out deep strike or infiltrate
poxwalkers_and_cheap_bodies.detail: Use them to create no-go zones where enemies cannot safely appear or charge without exposing themselves to Plague Marine counter-attacks.
Assign them to actions that might leave a model exposed or out of position, keeping Plague Marines free for fighting and durable scoring.
